data.Description: The ability to hide, sneak or steal.
data.Bonus_effect_1: Give a success to another Kid.
data.Bonus_effect_2:
You find something unexpected, or more of what
you were looking for.

data.Description: The ability to lift heavy things, fight, and endure physically stressful situations.
data.Bonus_effect_1: Give a success to another Kid.
data.Bonus_effect_5: You don’t need to roll to overcome the exact same Trouble in the future.

data.Description: The ability to climb high, balance, run fast, and chase someone or get away.
data.Bonus_effect_1: Give a success to another Kid.

data.Description:
The ability to build and manipulate machines and other mechanical items.
The Gamemaster will tell you what it takes to build something. Some examples of what might be needed:
• You need a certain item.
• You need to sucessfully Calculate
• You need to sucesfully Comprehend
• You need to sucessfully Programsomething
• You need a lot of time.
• You need new tools.

When you have what you need, you roll to overcome the Trouble of actually making the thing. If the roll is sucessful, you write down the object as an Item with a bonus of +1.
data.Bonus_effect_1: The thing is more durable than expected. Add +1 to the bonus (up to +3).
data.Bonus_effect_2: The thing can do more than expected. Add +1 to the bonus (up to +3).
data.Bonus_effect_3: The thing is more discreet than expected. Add +1 the bonus (up to +3).

data.Description: The ability to build and manipulate machines and other mechanical items. The Tinker skill can also be used to break, use, or jury-rig mechanical things, to pick locks, and drive motor vechicles. Sometimes you have to use Calculate first to figure out how to do it.
data.Bonus_effect_1: Give a success to another Kid.
data.Bonus_effect_2: You don’t need to roll to overcome the exact same Trouble in the future.

data.Description:
The ability to create and manipulate computer programs and electronic devices. This is a sister skill to Tinker, but used for electronic things rather than mechanical.
The Gamemaster will tell you what it takes to create something. Some examples of what might be needed:
•You need a certain item.
• You need to sucessfully Calculate
• You need to sucesfully Comprehend
• You need to sucessfully Tinkersomething
• You need a lot of time.
• You need new tool

When you have what you need, you roll to overcome the Trouble of actually creating the thing. If the roll is successful, write down the object as an Item with a bonus of +1.
data.Bonus_effect_1: The thing is more durable than expected. Add +1 to the bonus (up to +3).
data.Bonus_effect_2: The thing can do more than expected. Add +1 to the bonus (up to +3).

data.Description:
The ability to create and manipulate computer programs and electronic devices. This is a sister skill to Tinker, but used for electronic things rather than mechanical.
The Program skill can also be used to manipulate electronic items. Examples include infecting a computer with a virus, disabling an alarm or electronic lock, confusing or controlling robots, and operating strange objects like time machines or transformation globes. Sometimes you have to use calculate first to figure out how to do it.
data.Bonus_effect_1: Give a success to another Kid
data.Bonus_effect_2: You don’t need to roll to overcome the exact same Trouble in the future.
data.Bonus_effect_4: You get new or unexpected information.

data.Description:
The ability to know how technical objects work and how to use them. It could be robots, machines, magnetrine ships, cyborgs, or an alarm clock. If you succeed, you get to ask two questions:
• What is its purpose?
• How does it work?
• How can I use it?
• Who built it?
• What problems could it cause?
• Is it illegal?
data.Bonus_effect_1:
Ask one additional question and take +1 die on one
roll when you use the information (up to +3).

data.Description:
The ability to know the right person and can get a hold of
her. You tell the Gamemaster who the person is, and roll to
overcome the Trouble of finding her. If you succeed, you
find her, or she finds the Kids ready and able to help. If you
fail, the person doesn’t want to help you or maybe she even
wants to hurt, humiliate or make life difficult for you, and
will come looking for you.
data.Bonus_effect_1: The contact has all the right tools with her.
data.Bonus_effect_2: The contact may heal one of your conditions.
data.Bonus_effect_3: The contact brings more people, also ready to help
data.Bonus_effect_4: You don’t have to roll to get a hold of the contact again during this mystery
data.Bonus_effect_1: You may use the contact once as an item with a  bonus of +1 (up to +3).

data.Description:
The ability to charm, lie, befriend, and make people
do what you want. As always, the Gamemaster decides
what can plausibly be achieved,
data.Bonus_effect_2: You have formed a lasting relationship
data.Bonus_effect_3: She will take risks to help you.
data.Bonus_effect_4: She will try to persuade others to believe you.

data.Description:
The ability to make your friends work together, to help them focus on the task at hand in difficult situations, and to soothe them when they are scared or confused.
If you spend time privately with another Kid, giving her advice and comfort, you may heal one of her Conditions. You roll to overcome Trouble, and if you succeed, the Condition is healed. If you fail, you suffer the same Condition. You cannot heal Broken Kids using this skill.
data.Bonus_effect_2: Heal one of your own conditions.

data.Description:
The ability to make your friends work together, to help them focus on the task at hand in difficult situations, and to soothe them when they are scared or confused.
If you have time with the other Kids, you may inspire and prepare them for a difficult situation. You roll to overcome Trouble, and your successes become a dice pool (see the table below). You may, in the upcoming scenes, distribute bonus dice to the other Kids when they roll to overcome Troubles – but only if they do as you say. You cannot give these dice to yourself.
If you fail to lead, you must check a Condition, and ask the other Kids how the relationship has been hurt. There may only be one dice pool active at a time – the group cannot have more than one leader.
Leading Other Kids
SuccessesDice Pool
1 success2 dice
2 successes4 dice
3 successes4 dice

The ability to find hidden objects, understand clues, break puzzles or survey a place or a situation. You get to ask two of these questions:
• What is hidden here and where is it?
• What does it mean?
• What has happened here?
• How can I get into/out of/past something?
• What threats can I perceive here?
• Where is it?

If the roll fails, you have misunderstood something, someone has found out something about you, or you suffer a Condition. The Gamemaster decides.
data.Bonus_effect_1:
Ask one additional question and get +1 die on one
roll when you use the information (up to +3).

data.Description:
The ability to have the right piece of information or to be
able to find it at the school library or a similar location.
The Gamemaster will give you the information, or ask
you to come up with something on your own. If you fail
the roll, the Gamemaster gives you bad news or the wrong
information (the Gamemaster doesn’t say which it is).
data.Bonus_effect_1:
You get additional information and +1 bonus die to
one roll when you use the information (up to +3).

data.Description:
The ability to study what makes a person, an animal or a conscious robot or cyborg tick, and how to find its weakness. You need time to study or talk to the creature or person to be able to roll for the skill. If you succeed, you get to ask two questions.
•What is her weak spot?
•How can I make her do something
•What does she feel?
• What does she want?
• What will she do?
• s she lying?

If the roll fails, the Gamemaster either gives you bad news or the wrong information (the Gamemaster doesn’t tell which it is) or makes something bad happen.
data.Bonus_effect_1:
Ask one additional question and take +1 die on one
roll when you use the information (up to +3).
